notes: v325 — TWO QUICK TESTER FIXES. (1) The ladder now names the signal. The 🥇/🔷 split rungs used to read just "down-day", which didn't say WHICH flag and clashed with the 🥇 rank medal — confusing. They now read "🥇 Vol record · down day" / "🔷 Block day · up day", the rank is a plain gold/silver/bronze number, and the bearish flags (📉 Dist? and the down-day forms) carry a "· warning (predicts a fall)" tag so a green top-ranked WARNING is never mistaken for a buy — it just means that warning has been reading the falls correctly. (2) Announcements now line up with the price history. A tester saw news on a day with no price row, on every share — because the announcements list reached back weeks while the report's price table only showed the last ~10 sessions. Now the report's Recent sessions stretch back far enough to cover the announcements listed, so each 📢 sits on its own price row; anything older than that is noted with the 🏛 link. Announcement dates are also now read exactly as the ASX reports them (no timezone round-trip that could bump a pre-market lodgement to the day before). ALSO RECENT — v324 — 🪜 THE LADDER: a best-to-worst leaderboard at the end of the Signal report card. A tester asked to see, at a glance, which signals are winning right now — so the card now ends with two ranked ladders. ① Singles ranks the individual flags (Breakout, Informed?, Inst?, MA cross, Dist? and the up-day/down-day volume & block splits); ② Combinations & sequences ranks the pairs, triples and accumulation→ignition sequences (top 12). Everything is ranked by its EDGE in its own direction — the bearish flags (📉 Dist?, 🔴 down-day) score by how far price FELL, so a warning that's actually working ranks high and shows green. Each rung has a proportional bar, the edge, a 🥇🥈🥉 for the top three, and the SOLID/PROMISING/TOO EARLY badge — so a big edge that hasn't earned trust yet is still clearly flagged. It re-uses the same honest maths the rest of the card is built from — no new numbers, just a ranked view. As always: one good week proves nothing; the 📜 record across cards is what matters. ALSO RECENT — v323 — SEE WHICH HISTORY DAYS HAD AN ANNOUNCEMENT. Straight after v322: the day-by-day history now marks the days an ASX announcement was lodged with a 📢, so you can tell at a glance whether a volume spike lined up with news. It's on both the in-app 📊 share-detail history (it loads that share's announcements when you open it) and the printable report's Recent sessions table. Hover the 📢 for the headline; the tooltip flags ⚠ when the announcement was market-sensitive. It matches on the exact date, using the same real ASX feed as the list. One honest note: the feed only returns the latest ~20 announcements, so only recent days can be marked — a day without a 📢 isn't a promise nothing was lodged (the caption says so), and the 🏛 Company announcements link always has the full official history. ASX shares only. ALSO RECENT — v322 — COMPANY ANNOUNCEMENTS, NOW ON THE REPORT ITSELF. A tester asked: when you tap Company announcements a new tab opens — it'd be better to see the list of announcements with their dates right on the same page as the price/volume history. Done. The app already pulls REAL announcements from the official ASX feed (it uses them for the news flags and the report card's news-vs-quiet split) — it just wasn't showing you the list. Now the one-share report includes a 'Recent ASX announcements' table right under the recent-sessions history: each announcement's date and headline, with the market-sensitive ones marked ⚠. It loads that share's list automatically when you open the report (instant if it's already been checked today, a few seconds the first time for a new share). If the live feed can't be reached it says so and the official 🏛 Company announcements link is always still there for the full list. It's real ASX data — nothing is invented, and it's still a summary to verify, not advice. (ASX shares; the live feed is ASX-only.) ALSO RECENT — v321 — FIXED THE SLOWDOWN / 'PAGE UNRESPONSIVE'. A tester reported the app freezing, twice. Profiling a full 2,859-share market pinned the cause exactly: opening 📋 Signal report card re-graded EVERY signal over weeks of history from scratch on EVERY open (~0.7s on a fast desktop, several seconds on a phone), and reopening it froze the page again each time — which is precisely the 'Page Unresponsive, second time' that was seen. The auto-pilot already reused a once-a-day cache for the same maths; the report card didn't. Fixed two ways: the card now REUSES that once-a-day result, so after the first grade every reopen is instant; and on that first grade of the day it shows a 'Grading every signal…' note first, then does the work a moment later — so the tap never lands on a frozen screen. None of the grading numbers changed — it's the same honest maths, just computed once instead of every time. If you were stuck on an unresponsive page, hard-refresh (Ctrl+F5) to load this build. ALSO RECENT — v320 — 📉 DIP FINDER NOW SHOWS ON THE BUY SCREEN (not hidden behind a tab). Yesterday's v319 added the Dip Finder to the Buy dialog, but tucked it inside the Limit-buy section — so on the default 'Buy at market' tab it was invisible, and a tester couldn't find it. Fixed: the gold 📉 Dip Finder button now sits right under the Buy-at-market / Limit-buy toggle, visible on both tabs. Tap it and it measures that share's real daily dips and offers tap-to-use depths; pick one and the dialog now flips itself to a Limit order and drops the price straight into the box for you — because buying a dip IS a limit order below the close. One tap from 'what dip does this share make?' to a primed limit buy at a sensible price. ALSO RECENT — v319 — 📉 DIP FINDER, NOW INSIDE THE BUY SCREEN. Tester request: when you tap 🛒 Buy and choose a Limit buy, you no longer have to guess the price or leave the screen — a '📉 Dip Finder' button right under the price box measures THAT share's real behaviour (its actual daily dips over the last ~85 sessions) and shows its median dip plus tap-to-use price chips — its median depth, 2% and 3% — each with roughly how often a day dips that far. Tap a chip and the limit price fills itself, the how-many-shares preview updates, and nothing you typed is lost — the whole thing happens inside the Buy screen. Why it matters: one number never fits all shares — a 3-cent explorer swings 5–10% a day while a bank barely moves 1%, so a limit set by feel either never fills or fills too easily. Measuring the share you actually mean to buy replaces the guess with its own data. The full table — including the honest '+5 days after the fill' test that shows whether buying that dip actually paid — is still one tap away in ⚙ bot settings → 📉 Dip Finder. Estimates from end-of-day data, not advice. ALSO RECENT — v318 — THE COMBO GATE NOW SAYS WHAT IT WAS LOOKING FOR. A tester's diary read '… 🟢green 3 → 🧩combo 0' day after day — three shares survived every rule, then the combo gate said no, and the bare 'combo 0' looked like a fault. It wasn't: none of the three fired his chosen pair TOGETHER that day, which is exactly what a tight pair on a small pond does — pairs fire in clusters, and zero-days are proof the gate is selective, not broken. Two changes so the diary makes that obvious: the funnel stage now NAMES the requirement — '🧩🚀+🏦 0' instead of '🧩combo 0' (and '🧩📋 adaptive', '🧩🏦→🚀 chain', whichever you chose) — and a curated-pair zero now explains itself in plain words: how many shares reached the gate, why zero-days are normal, and the two levers if you want more daily action (set ⚙ source to the Smart Money list — a far bigger pond than the Top-20 — or switch the 🧩 combo gate off and let your signal ticks do the filtering). Rule of thumb from the maths: every gate you stack multiplies the odds down — a 20-share pond × quiet × RSI × day-jump × green × a same-day pair is a rare coincidence by design. Loosen until the bot trades daily, then tighten one gate at a time, watching the funnel to see what each rule really costs. (Older notes live in the Owner’s Manual changelog.)
